research_equation.old_success: ReAct-style LLM agents work well for short-horizon tasks with small context
research_equation.broken_assumption: Agents can process arbitrarily long accumulated context without degradation
research_equation.hard_setting: Long-horizon web search and deep research tasks with 35+ interaction steps
research_equation.borrowed_tool: External smaller LLM (Qwen3-4B-Instruct) as context manager + RL training (GRPO)
research_equation.ideal_unavailable_mechanism: Train the agent itself with adaptive context management capabilities
research_equation.surrogate_mechanism: External frozen-agent-compatible manager that learns to modify context via RL, discovering per-agent strategies

pass1_five_c_triage.category: Method — context management framework for LLM agents
pass1_five_c_triage.context: LLM agents for long-horizon tasks suffer from context degradation. Prior work (MEM1, IterResearch, ReSum, MemAct) requires agent-side training or fixed summarization.
pass1_five_c_triage.correctness: Plausible. Experiments on two benchmarks with 4-way comparisons. Controlled eval with ablated baselines.
contributions: AdaCoM framework: external trained manager for frozen agents
pass1_five_c_triage.clarity: Well-structured. Method, experiments, analysis, and transfer sections are logically connected.
pass1_five_c_triage.direction_promise: High. The hidden assumption that manager-agent style compatibility predicts transfer beyond capability is under-explored and attackable.

pass2_evidence_chain.key_finding: External trained context manager consistently improves frozen agents. Two distinct strategies emerge (tiered vs eager distillation) depending on agent capability.
figures: Figure 1: AdaCoM workflow diagram — external manager before each agent step
Figure 2: Failure mode shifts — AdaCoM reduces incorrect answers and early give-ups
Figure 3: Context length distribution shows tiered vs eager distillation strategies
Figure 4: Cross-agent transfer — 23/28 positive pairs, capability-proximity pattern
tables: Table 1: Main BCP results — AdaCoM +39% avg over ReAct
evidence_gaps: MCP-Wiki only uses 2 agents (limited agent pool)
No evaluation on code agents or embodied agents

implementation_details.training: SFT warmup (5 epochs on BCP, 2 on MCP) using GPT-5/Claude Opus trajectories, then GRPO with group size 8
implementation_details.action_space: JSON list of modifications with fields: ids, role, justification, new_content
implementation_details.process_rewards: Token penalty (-0.8), redundant-action penalty (-0.4), format penalty (-0.5), gold-doc-found (0.6), key-doc-found (0.3)
implementation_details.hyperparameters: α=0.1, lr=5e-6, KL=0.006, clip=0.2, temp=1.0, max 40 epochs

hidden_assumptions.assumption: 4B manager has sufficient capacity for all target agents
hidden_assumptions.breaks_at: Very strong agents (GPT-5 class) that need high-fidelity context preservation

hidden_assumptions.assumption: RL from frozen agent feedback provides sufficient training signal
hidden_assumptions.breaks_at: When agent is too weak to produce meaningful outcome variation

hidden_assumptions.assumption: JSON action space captures all useful context modifications
hidden_assumptions.breaks_at: Modifications requiring multi-turn coordination or planning

hidden_assumptions.assumption: Process rewards (token penalty, redundancy) generalize across tasks
hidden_assumptions.breaks_at: Tasks where long tool results are inherently necessary, not just noisy

hidden_assumptions.assumption: Capability proximity measured by ReAct is a sufficient transfer predictor
hidden_assumptions.breaks_at: When manager-agent style compatibility overrides capability signals

challenge_to_module_map.failure_mode: Constraint forgetting, premature abandonment, redundant exploration
challenge_to_module_map.module: AdaCoM workflow: manager modifies context before each agent step

challenge_to_module_map.challenge: Different agents need different management strategies
challenge_to_module_map.module: Flexible JSON action space with 4 fields (ids, role, justification, new_content)

challenge_to_module_map.design_principle: RL training with outcome + process rewards
challenge_to_module_map.module: GRPO + two-level advantage estimation + process rewards
Transfer experiment: 4 trained managers → 8 target agents

module_lens_table.failure_fixed: Cannot train closed-source agents; per-agent management costs
module_lens_table.ideal_unavailable: Context management capability built into the agent itself
module_lens_table.available_proxy: Smaller LLM that intercepts and modifies context before each agent step
module_lens_table.hidden_assumption: 4B manager has enough capacity for all agents
module_lens_table.risk: Capacity bottleneck for very strong agents; inference overhead
module_lens_table.future_point: Hierarchical management: small manager for routine compression, large manager for complex restructuring

module_lens_table.failure_fixed: Fixed summarization cannot adapt to per-agent needs
module_lens_table.ideal_unavailable: Optimal per-step context understood by design
module_lens_table.available_proxy: Learned per-step structured modification via RL exploration
module_lens_table.hidden_assumption: All useful modifications are local (per-step) and expressible as JSON
module_lens_table.risk: May miss modifications requiring global planning across many steps
module_lens_table.future_point: Plan-ahead context management: manager pre-commits to a modification strategy for the next K steps

module_lens_table.failure_fixed: Outcome-only RL is too sparse for long-horizon credit assignment
module_lens_table.ideal_unavailable: Dense per-step reward from human annotation
module_lens_table.available_proxy: Outcome reward + rule-based process rewards (token/redundancy/format penalties)
module_lens_table.hidden_assumption: Rule-based process rewards are good proxies for useful context management
module_lens_table.risk: Process rewards may learn to game the proxy instead of improving context quality
module_lens_table.future_point: Learned process reward model trained on human judgments of context quality

module_lens_table.failure_fixed: Single strategy fails across capability-diverse agents
module_lens_table.ideal_unavailable: Ground-truth optimal compression rate for each agent
module_lens_table.available_proxy: RL discovers capability-correlated strategies (tiered for strong, eager for weak)
module_lens_table.hidden_assumption: Agent capability (ReAct score) is the dominant predictor of optimal strategy
module_lens_table.risk: Style compatibility can override capability signal in transfer
module_lens_table.future_point: Adaptive strategy selection: detect target agent capability + style at inference time, select pre-trained manager

citation_function_table.function: Field anchor — real-world LLM agent applications
citation_function_table.modification: Uses these as motivation for long-horizon context problem

citation_function_table.cluster: Attention sink, Lost in the Middle, Distraction
citation_function_table.modification: Frame as central bottleneck for long-horizon agents
Shows these require agent-side training; AdaCoM removes this requirement
Adapted for manager training with two-level advantage

citation_function_table.inherited: Web search and deep research evaluation
citation_function_table.modification: Data splits (680/150 BCP; 1000 RL / 150 test MCP)
Follows same retriever setup for comparability

experiment_as_evidence_table.experiment_or_table: Table 1: BCP Main Results (4 agents × 6 settings)
experiment_as_evidence_table.supports: AdaCoM consistently improves diverse frozen agents
experiment_as_evidence_table.counterfactual_ruled_out: External manager without training fails; fixed summarization fails on some agents; self-management fails on most
experiment_as_evidence_table.stress_condition: 4 diverse agents (Qwen, Kimi, GLM, DeepSeek), multi-constraint web search

experiment_as_evidence_table.supports: AdaCoM reduces constraint forgetting, premature abandonment, redundant exploration
experiment_as_evidence_table.counterfactual_ruled_out: Improvement is not just more correct answers but also better failure trajectories

experiment_as_evidence_table.supports: Learned strategies differ systematically by agent capability
experiment_as_evidence_table.counterfactual_ruled_out: Strategies are emergent from RL, not hardcoded

experiment_as_evidence_table.supports: Managers transfer broadly; capability proximity helps; style compatibility matters
experiment_as_evidence_table.counterfactual_ruled_out: Managers are NOT narrowly tied to source agents
experiment_as_evidence_table.alignment: Strong for capability proximity; moderate for style compatibility

experiment_as_evidence_table.supports: AdaCoM extends to deep research tasks beyond web search
experiment_as_evidence_table.stress_condition: Deep research with 9 MCP tools, long-form synthesis

experiment_as_evidence_table.supports: Process rewards provide useful intermediate supervision

reviewer_lens_audit.novelty: Moderate-High. First systematic exploration of training an external context manager for frozen agents with flexible action space. Fidelity-Reliability Trade-off is a new empirical finding.
reviewer_lens_audit.significance: High for practical deployment. Directly addresses the real-world problem of applying context management to closed-source agents (OpenClaw, Hermes, Claude Code).
reviewer_lens_audit.soundness: Generally sound. Well-controlled experiments with multiple baselines. However, some concerns: (1) only 4 agents on BCP, 2 on MCP; (2) GPU hours and computational cost not reported; (3) no code agents tested.
reviewer_lens_audit.methodology_rigor: Good. Three runs reported (Mean@3 + Pass@3). Ablation on process rewards. Transfer analysis with 32 pairs. Exception analysis for failed transfers.
reviewer_lens_audit.statistical_validity: Adequate. Mean@3 over 3 runs gives some reliability. No confidence intervals or significance tests reported.
reviewer_lens_audit.reproducibility: Good. Code available (anonymous repo), detailed hyperparameters, dataset construction pipeline documented. SFT warmup data from GPT-5/Claude trajectories somewhat non-reproducible.
reviewer_lens_audit.limitation_honesty: Good. Explicit limitations section discussing evaluation scope, manager capacity, inference overhead, KV cache issues.

actionable_reviewer_objections.objection: Only one manager size (4B) tested — is scaling monotonic?
actionable_reviewer_objections.direction_seed: Train managers at 7B, 14B, 70B scales and measure Fidelity-Reliability boundary shift. Is there a 'sweet spot' where manager barely outperforms agent's native context window?
actionable_reviewer_objections.minimum_experiment: Compare 4B vs 14B manager on 2 strong agents (GLM, Qwen) on BCP with 3 seeds each

actionable_reviewer_objections.direction_seed: Frame-wise overhead analysis: measure and report latency, token cost, and cache invalidation overhead. Compare with sparser manager invocation (every N steps).

actionable_reviewer_objections.objection: Process reward ablation is thin (only α=0 vs α=0.1)
actionable_reviewer_objections.direction_seed: Sweep α systematically; also test alternative PR combinations (e.g. token-only, redundancy-only). Understand which process signals matter most.

story_pattern_to_reuse.compact_formula: "Agents can self-manage context" → false for closed-source → external manager learns per-agent strategy via RL
story_pattern_to_reuse.lesson: When the dominant approach requires access you don't have, find a surrogate that works from the outside — and use RL to discover task-specific patterns rather than hand-designing them.
